title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · Recovery Case: Gita Force With Lease Atomic
summary: A mirror, fork, upstream, deployment remote, and namespace-specific integration ref disagree after a rewrite; one stale client may overwrite reviewed work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita force with lease atomic increment in distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.
Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Force With Lease Atomic
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G91 is a theory in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Force With Lease Atomic contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use explicit force-with-lease expectations, force-if-includes, atomic push, dry-run, and signed-push evidence while accounting for background fetch.

content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G91 is a worked example in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push. A defensible solution applies Gita Force With Lease Atomic, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 4
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Force With Lease Atomic evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-force-with-lease-atomic-y7jyob" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Force With Lease Atomic into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-force-with-lease-atomic-y7jyob
# scenario-key: trace-y7jyob probe-boyj7y boundary-jyoby7 owner-y7jyobx fault-y7jyobz
# before-ref: main=a11cey7 candidate=b00b1ob
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11cey7 candidate=c0ffeeb
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs
